zh-tw.title: 世界盃熬夜語錄
zh-tw.description: 美加墨世界盃開踢，凌晨三點開賽、上午邊開會偷看——獻給每個為了不是自己國家的隊伍熬夜，隔天還要上班的真愛球迷。

en.title: World Cup Night-Owl Quotes
en.description: The 2026 World Cup kicks off across the US, Canada and Mexico. 3AM kickoffs, sneaking scores during the morning meeting — for every fan who stays up for a team that isn't even theirs, then shows up to work anyway.

items.sourceNote: 2026 世界盃開幕戰台灣時間 6/12 凌晨 03:00；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
開幕戰，台灣時間凌晨三點開踢。
真正的球迷，不是看得懂越位的人，
是願意三點爬起來、隔天還要照常上班的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個說好要陪你看開幕戰、結果十一點就先睡著的朋友
variations: 凌晨三點的世界盃，篩選的從來不是球技，是愛。
懂不懂足球不重要，敢不敢三點起床才是門檻。

en.content:
Opening match: 3 AM kickoff, local time.
A real fan isn't the one who understands offside —
it's the one who drags themselves up at 3 and still clocks in the next morning.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who swore they'd watch the opener with you, then fell asleep at 11
variations: A 3 AM World Cup filters for one thing, and it was never skill. It's love.
Knowing the rules is optional. Getting up at 3 is the real entry fee.

items.sourceNote: 美西晚場落台灣上午 09:00-10:00「早餐球」register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆世界盃對台灣最佛的地方，
是有些場次落在早上九點、十點。
別人叫它美西晚場，我們叫它「早餐球」——
配蛋餅，配豆漿，配一點點遲到的勇氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個在早餐店看球看到忘記要去上班的同事
variations: 早餐球的精髓：邊吃蛋餅邊看球，邊算還來不來得及打卡。
美西的深夜，是我們的早餐時間。時差第一次站在台灣這邊。

en.content:
The kindest thing about this World Cup for us:
some matches land at 9 or 10 in the morning.
The rest of the world calls it a late West Coast game.
We call it breakfast football — eggs, soy milk, and a little courage to be late.
en.editorNote: For the coworker who got so deep into a match at the breakfast shop they forgot to go to work
variations: Breakfast football: one eye on the score, one eye on whether you can still make it in on time.
Their midnight is our breakfast. For once, the time zones are on our side.

items.sourceNote: 上午場卡上班時間 邊開會偷看 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
上午十點那場，剛好卡在我的晨會。
老闆在台上講這季的目標，
我在筆電後面，用另一個視窗追比分。
我們兩個都很緊張，只是緊張的東西不太一樣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個開會時筆電永遠開兩個視窗的戰友
variations: 晨會最長的不是議程，是我這邊的比賽還沒進球。
視訊鏡頭關掉的那一刻，我的世界盃才正式開賽。

en.content:
The 10 AM match lands right on top of my morning standup.
The boss is up front talking quarterly targets.
I'm behind my laptop, tracking the score in another window.
We're both tense — just about completely different things.
en.editorNote: For the comrade whose laptop always has exactly two windows open during meetings
variations: The longest part of a standup isn't the agenda. It's my match still being 0-0.
The moment my camera turns off, my real World Cup kicks off.

items.sourceNote: 「半友善世界盃」不必全程熬夜 但淘汰賽仍凌晨 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
大家說這是「半友善世界盃」，不用全程熬夜。
我信了。
直到淘汰賽，最精彩的那幾場，又準時排回凌晨四點。
原來「半友善」的意思是：小組賽對你客氣，淘汰賽對你現出原形。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個小組賽都睡飽、淘汰賽開始崩潰的朋友
variations: 半友善的「半」，指的是它只在你不在乎的場次對你好。
越想看的比賽，時間排得越深夜，這是世界盃的潛規則。

en.content:
They called it the 'half-friendly World Cup' — you won't have to stay up the whole way.
I believed them.
Then the knockouts came, and the best matches slid right back to 4 AM.
'Half-friendly,' it turns out, means: polite in the group stage, ruthless in the knockouts.
en.editorNote: For the friend who slept fine through the group stage and fell apart once the knockouts began
variations: The 'half' in half-friendly refers to the games you don't actually care about.
The match you want most always gets scheduled deepest into the night. It's a law.

items.sourceNote: 2026 史上首屆 48 隊 104 場 死亡之組 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆四十八隊，史上最多，
新聞說幾乎每一組都是死亡之組。
我看著自己這個月的待辦清單，
默默覺得，被分進死亡之組的，其實是我。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個行事曆滿到自己都不敢點開的同事
variations: 四十八隊裡總有人能晉級，我的待辦清單裡沒有。
別人的死亡之組六月就結束，我的死亡之組從來沒有賽程表。

en.content:
Forty-eight teams this year — the most ever.
The headlines say nearly every group is a group of death.
I look at my own to-do list for the month
and quietly realize the one stuck in the group of death is me.
en.editorNote: For the coworker whose calendar is so full they're afraid to open it
variations: Out of forty-eight teams, someone always advances. My to-do list has no such mercy.
Their group of death ends in June. Mine never even got a fixture list.

items.sourceNote: 愛爾達 VOD 隔天補看 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
錯過凌晨那場沒關係，隔天還能看重播。
於是我通勤的捷運上補的，不只是比賽，
還有昨晚為了它失去的睡眠。
球補得回來，黑眼圈補不回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個用 VOD 把世界盃看成連續劇的朋友
variations: VOD 補看的真相：補的是球，欠的是命。
重播能倒帶，我的生理時鐘不行。

en.content:
Missed the 3 AM match? No problem, there's a replay tomorrow.
So on my morning commute I'm catching up — not just on the game,
but on the sleep I lost for it last night.
The match, I can replay. The eye bags, I cannot.
en.editorNote: For the friend bingeing the World Cup on replay like it's a TV drama
variations: The truth about catching up on VOD: you reclaim the goals, you still owe the sleep.
A replay can rewind. My body clock cannot.

items.sourceNote: Tayo Ricci「World Cup」everybody jump 迷因；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
今年的世界盃應援曲一出來，全網都在喊「everybody jump」。
我聽完只有一個感想：
大家都跳，可以，但我選擇躺平。
這首歌唯一讓我熱血的部分，是大家一起罵它的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個被那首神曲洗腦、邊罵邊跟著哼的朋友
variations: 「everybody jump」——除了我，我這邊維持躺平。
一首歌能把全世界團結起來一起嫌棄，某種程度上也算成功了。

en.content:
The official anthem dropped and the whole internet started yelling 'everybody jump.'
I listened, and had exactly one thought:
everybody jump, sure — but I'm choosing to lie flat.
The only part of this song that fires me up is watching everyone roast it together.
en.editorNote: For the friend who can't stop humming the cursed anthem while complaining about it
variations: 'Everybody jump' — except me. I'll be over here, horizontal.
A song that unites the entire world in collectively hating it is, in a way, a success.

items.sourceNote: 史上最爛世界盃歌 還是循環播放 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
公認史上最難聽的世界盃歌，
我已經循環播放第四十遍。
不是因為好聽，
是因為它跟這屆熬夜一樣——明知道很痛苦，卻怎麼也戒不掉。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個歌單裡偷偷加了那首神曲、不敢承認的人
variations: 難聽到我恨它，洗腦到我循環它，這就是世界盃的魔力。
我跟這首歌的關係，跟我跟凌晨三點的關係一樣——又愛又恨，戒不掉。

en.content:
The most hated World Cup song ever recorded,
and I'm on my fortieth loop.
Not because it's good —
but because it's just like staying up for this tournament: I know it hurts, and I still can't quit it.
en.editorNote: For the person who quietly added the cursed anthem to their playlist and won't admit it
variations: Bad enough to hate, catchy enough to loop. That's the magic of the World Cup.
My relationship with this song is my relationship with 3 AM: love it, hate it, can't quit it.

items.sourceNote: 看球比上班準時 自律用錯地方 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
我上班從來沒準時過，
但凌晨三點的開賽，我提前十五分鐘就坐好了。
原來我不是沒有自律，
我只是把自律，全部用在最不該用的地方。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個鬧鐘按十次、卻能為球賽自動醒來的朋友
variations: 我的生理時鐘認得開賽時間，認不得打卡時間。
能凌晨三點精準起床的人，要相信自己其實很有潛力。

en.content:
I've never once been on time for work,
but for a 3 AM kickoff I'm seated and ready fifteen minutes early.
Turns out it's not that I lack discipline —
I just spend all of it in the worst possible place.
en.editorNote: For the friend who snoozes the alarm ten times but wakes up automatically for the match
variations: My body clock recognizes kickoff time. It does not recognize the time I'm due at work.
Anyone who can wake up precisely at 3 AM should believe in their own potential.

items.sourceNote: 不懂越位 跟著全場一起罵 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
朋友問我剛剛裁判為什麼吹哨。
我說：「越位。」
他說：「你看得懂越位喔？」
我說：「看不懂，但全場都站起來罵，我就跟著站起來。」
世界盃最團結的一刻，就是全場一起對一個我們都看不懂的判決生氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟你一樣，每次都比裁判晚半秒才知道發生什麼事的朋友
variations: 判越位的是 VAR，跟著生氣的是不懂越位的我們。
看不懂沒關係，會在對的時間握拳，你就是這個夜晚的一份子。

en.content:
My friend asked why the ref just blew the whistle.
I said, 'Offside.'
He said, 'You can read offside?'
I said, 'No idea, but the whole stadium stood up to complain, so I stood up too.'
The most united moment of the World Cup is everyone getting angry together at a call none of us understood.
en.editorNote: For the friend who, like you, always figures out what happened half a second after everyone else
variations: VAR makes the offside call. The rest of us, who can't read offside, supply the outrage.
You don't have to understand it. Clench your fist at the right moment and you belong here.

items.sourceNote: ELTA 季租 499 vs 熬夜代價 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
轉播季票四百九十九，我一開始覺得有點貴。
後來算了一下：一個月的咖啡、提神飲料、隔天上班渙散掉的精神……
四百九十九，其實是這整個夏天裡，我花得最不心痛的一筆。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在猶豫要不要訂季票、卻已經為它失眠的朋友
variations: 季票不貴，貴的是看完之後那條回不去的作息。
省下四百九十九的人，通常會用咖啡錢加倍奉還。

en.content:
A season pass costs about fifteen bucks, and at first I thought that was steep.
Then I did the math: a month of coffee, energy drinks, and all the focus I lose at work the next day…
Fifteen bucks turns out to be the least painful thing I'll spend all summer.
en.editorNote: For the friend still hesitating over the season pass while already losing sleep over it
variations: The pass isn't expensive. The sleep schedule you'll never get back is.
Whoever skips the pass usually pays it back double in coffee.

items.sourceNote: 半夜進球歡呼吵到鄰居/室友 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
凌晨三點，進球的瞬間我忍不住大叫。
三秒後，隔壁牆壁傳來「咚咚」兩聲。
我們從來沒講過話，但那一刻我們達成了共識：
他支持的不是哪一隊，是睡眠。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜看球被鄰居或室友抗議過的人，你們是同一種人
variations: 進球的歡呼有多大聲，隔天的道歉就要多小聲。
世界盃期間，最了解你作息的不是家人，是隔壁那道牆。

en.content:
3 AM. The goal goes in and I can't help shouting.
Three seconds later, two knocks come through the wall.
We've never spoken, but in that moment we reach an understanding:
the team he supports isn't a team. It's sleep.
en.editorNote: For anyone who's been shushed by a neighbor or roommate during a late match — you're the same breed
variations: However loud the goal celebration, the next-morning apology has to be twice as quiet.
During the World Cup, the one who knows your schedule best isn't family. It's the wall next door.

items.sourceNote: 為了不是自己國家的隊伍熬夜 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
最浪漫的事，
是為了一支不在我國家、我也叫不全名字的球隊，
在凌晨四點握緊拳頭，比為自己的人生還認真。
世界盃教我的事：原來人可以為了一件無關緊要的事，全力以赴。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配開賽前的限動，獻給每個莫名其妙就有了主隊的人
variations: 我為我的人生從沒這麼緊張過，卻為一群陌生人緊張到手心冒汗。
選主隊不需要理由，就像愛上一個人不需要報備。

en.content:
The most romantic thing
is staying up till 4 AM, fist clenched,
for a team that isn't from my country and whose roster I can't even name —
trying harder than I ever do for my own life.
The World Cup taught me: a person really can give their all to something that doesn't matter.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a pre-match story post — for everyone who somehow ended up with a team to root for
variations: I've never been this nervous about my own life, yet here I am, palms sweating over strangers.
You don't need a reason to pick a team, the same way you don't need permission to fall for someone.

items.sourceNote: 熬夜看球 黑眼圈 隔天上班 register；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
世界盃期間的我，分成兩個版本：
凌晨三點，精神抖擻，扯著嗓子歡呼。
早上九點，行屍走肉，扶著牆壁進公司。
同事問我怎麼了，我只能說：
「我沒事，是我的隊昨天有點事。」
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個頂著黑眼圈來上班、還在偷查昨晚比分的同事
variations: 凌晨的我和上班的我，是兩個簽了切結書互不認識的人。
黑眼圈是世界盃球迷的識別證，不用說，大家都認得。

en.content:
During the World Cup, I come in two versions:
3 AM — wide awake, screaming my lungs out.
9 AM — a walking corpse, holding the wall on my way into the office.
A coworker asks what's wrong, and all I can say is:
'I'm fine. My team had a rough night.'
en.editorNote: For the coworker who showed up with eye bags and is still secretly checking last night's score
variations: 3 AM me and 9 AM me are two people who signed a contract to never acknowledge each other.
Eye bags are a World Cup fan's ID badge. No words needed — everyone recognizes them.

items.sourceNote: 決賽 7/19 美東 15:00，台灣時間 7/20 凌晨 03:00；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
整個夏天熬下來，最後一場決賽，
台灣時間還是落在凌晨三點。
從開幕熬到冠軍，這顆球陪我度過了一個失眠的夏天。
捨不得它結束，不是因為比賽，
是因為結束之後，我又得回去當一個正常作息的人。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合決賽夜的限動，獻給每個又期待又捨不得這個夏天結束的球迷
variations: 決賽哨音響起的那一刻，我才發現我捨不得的不是比賽，是熬夜也甘願的那個自己。
從開幕的三點到決賽的三點，這個夏天我活在另一個時區。

en.content:
After a whole summer of staying up, the final
still lands at 3 AM, local time.
From the opener to the trophy, this ball kept me company through one sleepless summer.
I don't want it to end — not because of the football,
but because once it's over, I have to go back to being a person with a normal sleep schedule.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a final-night story post — for every fan who's excited and heartbroken that the summer is ending
variations: When the final whistle blew, I realized what I'd miss wasn't the matches — it was the version of me who gladly stayed up for them.
From the 3 AM opener to the 3 AM final, I spent this whole summer living in another time zone.

items.sourceNote: 墨西哥城開幕戰 墨西哥 vs 南非 Estadio Azteca；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
開幕戰在墨西哥城的阿茲特克球場，
地主對南非。
說真的，這兩隊我一個球員都叫不出來。
但凌晨三點，我還是坐在電視前，
為兩支跟我毫無關係的隊伍，緊張得像在等放榜。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個開幕戰沒有主隊、卻照樣準時報到的朋友
variations: 認不認識球員不重要，世界盃開幕的氣氛，本身就值得熬這一夜。
我跟阿茲特克球場的距離，是一個時區加一整片太平洋，但今晚我們同步心跳。

en.content:
The opening match is at the Estadio Azteca in Mexico City,
the hosts against South Africa.
Honestly, I can't name a single player on either side.
But at 3 AM I'm parked in front of the TV anyway,
as nervous for two teams I have nothing to do with as if I were waiting on my own exam results.
en.editorNote: For the friend with no team in the opener who still shows up right on time
variations: Not knowing the players doesn't matter. The opening-night atmosphere alone is worth the lost sleep.
I'm a time zone and an entire ocean away from the Azteca, but tonight our heartbeats are synced.

items.sourceNote: 美加墨跨四個時區 賽程混亂 台灣時差 12-15 小時；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆橫跨北美四個時區，賽程亂到不行。
我手機裡多了三個時鐘、兩張賽程表、一個倒數 app，
還是常常搞不清楚下一場到底幾點。
別人在追比分，我在追的是——這場到底是今天還是昨天。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個手機裡裝了一堆世界盃 app、卻還是會睡過頭的朋友
variations: 看球之前要先算數學，這是美加墨世界盃才有的隱藏關卡。
時差讓我學會的事：先確認是哪一天，再來緊張幾比幾。

en.content:
This tournament sprawls across four North American time zones, and the schedule is pure chaos.
My phone now has three clocks, two fixture lists, and a countdown app,
and I still can't reliably tell you when the next match starts.
Everyone else is chasing the score. I'm chasing the answer to: is this game today, or yesterday?
en.editorNote: For the friend who installed five World Cup apps and still oversleeps the kickoff
variations: Doing the math before you can watch the game — that's the hidden boss level of a North American World Cup.
What the time difference taught me: confirm which day it is first, then start panicking over the scoreline.

items.sourceNote: 48 隊 104 場 史上最長賽程 一個多月；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆一百零四場，比以前多了一大截，
從六月中一路踢到七月底。
朋友問我暑假有什麼計畫，
我打開賽程表給他看：「這就是我的暑假計畫，一場都不能少。」
別人放暑假是休息，我放暑假是進入長期抗戰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個把整個夏天行事曆都讓給世界盃的朋友
variations: 一百零四場是別人的數據，是我接下來一個月的生理時鐘。
暑假計畫只有一個字：撐。撐到最後一場決賽。

en.content:
This edition has a hundred and four matches — way more than before,
running from mid-June all the way to late July.
A friend asked what my summer plans were.
I pulled up the fixture list and said, 'This is my summer plan. Not a single match skipped.'
Other people take summer off to rest. I take it off to begin a war of attrition.
en.editorNote: For the friend who handed their entire summer calendar over to the World Cup
variations: A hundred and four matches is a stat to everyone else. To me it's my body clock for the next month.
My summer plan is one word: endure. All the way to the final.

items.sourceNote: 開幕 AI 直播吸 142 萬人 「黃仁勳要的世界?」大象闖球場迷因；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
開幕的 AI 直播一百多萬人在看，
畫面裡突然冒出一隻大象闖進球場，
竟然還真的有人信。
網友在底下狂刷：「黃仁勳，這就是你要的世界嗎？」
凌晨三點，我分不清是 AI 太強，還是我太睏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟你一起被 AI 開幕畫面整到懷疑人生的朋友
variations: AI 生成的開幕畫面，比賽還沒開始我已經先看到大象進場。
熬夜到三點的好處：真假已經分不出來，看什麼都覺得合理。

en.content:
Over a million people tuned into the AI-generated opening livestream,
and then an elephant just strolled onto the pitch —
and somehow people actually believed it.
The comments lit up: 'Is this the world you wanted, Jensen?'
At 3 AM I genuinely can't tell whether the AI is too good or I'm just too sleepy.
en.editorNote: Send to the friend who was gaslit alongside you by the AI opening footage
variations: The AI opening clip had an elephant walking out before kickoff even happened.
The upside of staying up till 3: you can't tell real from fake, so everything looks plausible.

items.sourceNote: 家裡變成小型體育場 每球大叫；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
世界盃一開打，我家客廳就升級成小型體育場。
沙發是看台，茶几是球門，零食是中場補給。
每進一球我就跳起來大叫，
直到家人從房間探出頭，用眼神提醒我：
這裡的觀眾，只有我一個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一個人在客廳看球、卻嗨得像現場有兩萬人的朋友
variations: 客廳變體育場的代價，是全家人都被迫成為我的鄰座觀眾。
一個人的看台，照樣可以喊出兩萬人的聲量。

en.content:
The moment the World Cup starts, my living room gets promoted to a small stadium.
The couch is the stands, the coffee table is the goal, the snacks are halftime supplies.
Every goal, I leap up and scream —
until a family member pokes their head out and reminds me with a look
that the entire crowd here is just me.
en.editorNote: For the friend watching alone in the living room but hyped like there are twenty thousand people there
variations: The price of turning your living room into a stadium: your whole family becomes your unwilling seatmates.
A crowd of one can still roar at the volume of twenty thousand.

items.sourceNote: 群組變國際法庭 為比賽吵架；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
世界盃期間，我的家族群組變成國際法庭。
平常已讀不回的長輩，
為了一個越位判決，連發二十則語音，引經據典。
我們從來沒為任何家事這麼認真討論過，
卻能為一支大家都不是本國的球隊，吵到天亮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個平常潛水、世界盃期間突然在群組變話癆的家人
variations: 群組裡最熱絡的時刻，永遠是有人開始為一顆球翻臉的時候。
為國家大事我們沉默，為一個越位我們各執一詞到天亮。

en.content:
During the World Cup, my family group chat turns into an international tribunal.
The relatives who usually leave me on read
fire off twenty voice messages over one offside call, citing precedent.
We've never debated an actual family matter this passionately,
yet here we are, arguing till dawn over a team none of us is even from.
en.editorNote: For the relative who lurks all year and suddenly won't stop talking in the group chat during the World Cup
variations: The group chat is never livelier than the moment someone loses it over a single goal.
On the big stuff we stay silent. On one offside, we argue our cases until sunrise.

items.sourceNote: 餐飲業推熬夜球迷專屬宵夜套餐；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
現在連外送都推出「熬夜球迷專屬宵夜套餐」了。
凌晨兩點下單，三點開賽前剛好送到，
配著比賽吃，整個夜晚都被照顧到。
世界盃最厲害的地方，是它連你的胃，
都安排進了凌晨三點的賽程表裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個半夜看球一定要配宵夜、沒吃就看不下去的朋友
variations: 宵夜套餐和開賽時間同步推出，這座城市懂熬夜球迷的心。
比賽餵飽我的熱血，宵夜餵飽我的胃，凌晨三點原來這麼忙。

en.content:
Now even delivery apps have a 'night-owl fan' late-night combo.
Order at 2 AM, and it shows up right before the 3 AM kickoff,
so you eat through the match and the whole night feels taken care of.
The scariest part of the World Cup is that it's slotted even your stomach
into the 3 AM fixture list.
en.editorNote: For the friend who can't watch a late match without a midnight snack lined up first
variations: Midnight combos launched in sync with kickoff times — this city gets the night-owl fan.
The match feeds my adrenaline, the snack feeds my stomach. 3 AM is a busy hour.

items.sourceNote: 世界盃唯一讓人覺得凌晨四點起床很快樂的時候；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
世界盃是一年裡唯一一段時間，
讓人覺得凌晨四點爬起來是一件快樂的事。
平常這個時間醒著，叫失眠;
世界盃期間醒著，叫熱愛。
同一個時鐘，同一個四點，差別只在於——今晚有沒有比賽。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個平常四點失眠很痛苦、世界盃期間四點起床卻甘之如飴的朋友
variations: 凌晨四點清醒，平常是病，世界盃期間是信仰。
讓四點變得值得期待的，從來不是鬧鐘，是賽程表。

en.content:
The World Cup is the only stretch of the year
that makes getting up at 4 AM feel like a happy thing.
Normally, being awake at this hour is called insomnia.
During the World Cup, it's called devotion.
Same clock, same 4 AM — the only difference is whether there's a match tonight.
en.editorNote: For the friend who suffers at 4 AM all year but happily rises at 4 during the World Cup
variations: Awake at 4 AM is an illness most of the year and a religion during the World Cup.
What makes 4 AM worth looking forward to was never the alarm. It's the fixture list.

items.sourceNote: 澳洲 2-0 爆冷擊敗土耳其；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
澳洲二比零把土耳其爆冷掉的那一晚，
我明明誰都不支持，卻在沙發上跳起來大叫。
世界盃最迷人的地方，
從來不是強隊照劇本贏球，
是那支沒人看好的隊伍，把所有人的預測撕成碎片。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個專門喜歡幫弱隊加油、爆冷時最興奮的朋友
variations: 我熬夜不是為了看強隊贏，是為了等那一個沒人想到的爆冷。
預測表上寫得越死，被推翻時就越過癮，這就是世界盃。

en.content:
The night Australia stunned Türkiye 2-0,
I wasn't rooting for anyone, yet I leapt off the couch screaming.
The most thrilling thing about the World Cup
was never the favorites winning to script —
it's the team nobody believed in shredding everyone's predictions to confetti.
en.editorNote: For the friend who always cheers for the underdog and lives for an upset
variations: I don't stay up to watch the favorites win. I stay up for the upset nobody saw coming.
The more certain the bracket looked, the sweeter it is when it gets torn up. That's the World Cup.

items.sourceNote: 巴西 vs 摩洛哥 1-1 互交白卷的平手；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
巴西對摩洛哥，賽前我以為是一面倒，
結果一比一收場，我也熬到天快亮。
為了一場平手失去整夜睡眠，
我跟那場比賽一樣——
投入了所有，最後誰也沒贏。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個熬夜看完平手、隔天頂著黑眼圈問「我到底圖什麼」的朋友
variations: 一比一的平手，是兩隊都不甘心，加上我也沒睡飽。
看完平手的那種空虛，跟加班到半夜結果專案延期一模一樣。

en.content:
Brazil versus Morocco — I figured it'd be a blowout.
It finished 1-1, and I stayed up almost to sunrise for it.
Losing a whole night's sleep over a draw,
I'm just like that match:
I gave everything, and in the end nobody won.
en.editorNote: For the friend who watched a draw till dawn and asked the next morning, eye bags and all, 'what was I even doing'
variations: A 1-1 draw: two teams left unsatisfied, plus me running on no sleep.
The emptiness after a draw is exactly like grinding past midnight only to have the project slip anyway.

items.sourceNote: 開幕 LISA 登台、決賽 BTS/Shakira/Madonna 中場秀卡司；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆連卡司都豪華到犯規，
開幕請 LISA 登台，決賽還有 BTS、夏奇拉、瑪丹娜。
我朋友本來說對足球沒興趣，
看到卡司名單，立刻把鬧鐘調到凌晨三點。
世界盃最強的地方，是它連不看球的人，都找得到熬夜的理由。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個不為比賽、純為中場秀卡司熬夜的追星朋友
variations: 有人為球熬夜，有人為偶像熬夜，凌晨三點的客廳大家各有所圖。
看不懂足球沒關係，看得懂卡司就夠你撐到天亮了。

en.content:
Even the lineup this year is unfairly stacked:
LISA at the opener, then BTS, Shakira, and Madonna at the final.
My friend swore she had zero interest in soccer —
one look at the lineup and she set her alarm for 3 AM.
The World Cup's real power is giving even non-fans a reason to stay up.
en.editorNote: For the K-pop friend staying up not for the match but purely for the halftime lineup
variations: Some stay up for the football, some for their idols. At 3 AM, everyone in the living room has an angle.
You don't have to read the game. Reading the lineup is enough to keep you up till dawn.

items.sourceNote: 地主美國隊大勝巴拉圭、加拿大逼平波士尼亞；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
三個地主裡，美國首戰就痛電巴拉圭，
加拿大則跟波士尼亞握手言和。
身在台灣的我跟這三隊都沒淵源，
卻在凌晨默默幫地主緊張，
好像只要主辦國踢得好，這場我熬的夜就比較值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明沒有主隊、卻會自動幫地主加油的朋友
variations: 幫地主加油不需要理由，反正都是別人的國家，挑個踢得好看的就對了。
地主贏球的夜晚，連我的黑眼圈都覺得有面子。

en.content:
Of the three hosts, the U.S. throttled Paraguay in its opener,
while Canada shook hands on a draw with Bosnia.
Sitting in Taiwan, I have no ties to any of them,
yet at 3 AM I quietly root for the hosts,
as if a strong showing from the home nations makes my lost sleep more worth it.
en.editorNote: For the friend with no team of their own who automatically pulls for the host nations
variations: You don't need a reason to back the hosts. They're all someone else's country anyway, so pick the one that plays the prettiest.
On a night the hosts win, even my eye bags feel a little proud.

items.sourceNote: 德國 7-1 大勝庫拉索 一面倒比分；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
德國七比一痛宰庫拉索的那一晚，
我撐到第四球才發現，比分早就沒有懸念。
問題是，七顆球分散在九十分鐘裡進，
等於我為了一場早就分出勝負的比賽，把整個凌晨都賠了進去。
世界盃最殘忍的是：你永遠不知道，撐到最後的會是經典還是垃圾時間。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個熬夜看一面倒比賽、看到一半開始懷疑人生的朋友
variations: 七比一的比賽，前面是球迷，後面是觀光客，我兩種身分都當過了。
比分越懸殊，越考驗你到底是真愛還是只是睡不著。

en.content:
The night Germany hammered Curaçao 7-1,
I made it to the fourth goal before realizing the result had been settled long ago.
The catch is, seven goals spread across ninety minutes
means I traded my entire predawn for a match that was over before it started.
The cruelest thing about the World Cup: you never know until the end whether you stayed up for a classic or for garbage time.
en.editorNote: For the friend who stays up for a blowout and starts questioning their life halfway through
variations: A 7-1 match: a fan at the start, a tourist by the end. I played both roles tonight.
The more lopsided the score, the harder it tests whether you're a true fan or just an insomniac.

items.sourceNote: 荷蘭 2-2 日本 互有來回的精彩平手；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
荷蘭跟日本踢成二比二，來回拉鋸到最後一秒。
這種平手跟那種互交白卷的平手完全不一樣——
四顆球，兩次落後兩次追平，看完我累得像自己上場踢了九十分鐘。
原來不是所有平手都讓人空虛，
有些平手，是兩隊一起把整夜的睡眠變得超級值得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個熬夜看完精彩平手、隔天黑眼圈卻笑得很滿足的朋友
variations: 二比二也能讓我心甘情願睡四小時，這就是世界盃的本事。
好的平手不是沒人贏，是兩隊一起贏走了我整夜的心跳。

en.content:
Netherlands and Japan finished 2-2, trading blows down to the last second.
This kind of draw is nothing like a goalless stalemate —
four goals, twice behind and twice level, and by the end I was as wiped as if I'd played the ninety minutes myself.
Turns out not every draw leaves you empty.
Some draws are two teams teaming up to make a whole night of lost sleep absolutely worth it.
en.editorNote: For the friend who watched a thrilling draw and showed up the next day with eye bags and a satisfied grin
variations: A 2-2 that makes me happily run on four hours of sleep — that's what the World Cup can do.
A good draw isn't nobody winning. It's both teams winning my heartbeat for the whole night.

items.sourceNote: 象牙海岸 1-0 爆冷擊敗厄瓜多；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
象牙海岸一比零把被看好的厄瓜多送走，
那顆球進的瞬間，連我這個賽前根本不認識他們的人都跳起來。
爆冷的迷人之處在於：
它不是強隊照本宣科地贏，
是一支沒人押注的隊伍，用一顆球證明——這就是為什麼足球不能只看紙上排名。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個押錯隊、卻反而看得超開心的朋友
variations: 厄瓜多被爆冷的那一刻，全世界的預測表一起變成廢紙。
我熬夜不是為了驗證強弱，是為了看排名在球場上被推翻。

en.content:
Ivory Coast sent the fancied Ecuador packing 1-0,
and the instant that goal went in, even I — who couldn't have named the team before kickoff — leapt up.
The charm of an upset is this:
it isn't the favorite winning by the book,
it's a team nobody bet on proving, with a single goal, exactly why football can't be read off a ranking sheet.
en.editorNote: For the friend who picked the wrong team and ended up having the best night anyway
variations: The moment Ecuador got upset, every prediction sheet on earth turned to scrap paper.
I don't stay up to confirm who's better. I stay up to watch the rankings get overturned on the pitch.

items.sourceNote: 48 隊新賽制 8 個最佳第三名也能晉級；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆新賽制，連小組第三名都有機會晉級，
只要你是八個成績最好的第三名之一。
看著這個規則，我突然有點感動：
原來不是只有第一第二才有未來，
有時候你只要不墊底、再撐久一點，命運就會幫你留一個位子。
我決定把這句話，當成我這個月上班的座右銘。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個事情做不到頂尖、但靠穩定和不放棄活下來的同路人
variations: 最佳第三名的意思是：你不用最強，只要在該撐住的時候沒倒下。
人生跟新賽制一樣，給了第三名一條活路，就看你撐不撐得到那一刻。

en.content:
Under the new format, even a third-place team can advance —
as long as you're one of the eight best third-placed sides.
Looking at that rule, I got unexpectedly emotional:
turns out it's not only first and second who get a future.
Sometimes you just have to avoid finishing last and hold on a little longer, and fate saves you a seat.
I've decided to make that my motto at work this month.
en.editorNote: For the comrade who isn't top-tier at anything but survives on consistency and refusing to quit
variations: Being a best third-place team means: you don't have to be the strongest, just don't fall down when it counts.
Life is like the new format — it leaves the third-placed a way out. The question is whether you last long enough to take it.

items.sourceNote: 南韓 2-1 逆轉捷克 亞洲球隊表現；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
南韓二比一逆轉捷克的那場，
身為亞洲人的我，莫名其妙就站到了同一邊。
明明國籍不同、語言不通，
但只要是亞洲球隊在歐洲強權面前不認輸，
我這邊的拳頭就先握緊了。
世界盃神奇的地方：它讓你發現，原來你心裡偷偷裝著一整片大陸。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個只要亞洲球隊上場就自動切換成同鄉模式的朋友
variations: 球員我一個都不認識，但那面亞洲的旗，我認得。
逆轉那一刻我才懂，原來認同不需要護照，只需要一顆一起緊張的心。

en.content:
The night South Korea came back to beat Czechia 2-1,
something in me, as an Asian, just instinctively picked a side.
Different nationality, no shared language —
but the moment an Asian team refuses to back down against a European power,
my fist clenches before I even decide to.
The magic of the World Cup: it shows you that you've been quietly carrying a whole continent in your chest.
en.editorNote: For the friend who flips into hometown mode the instant any Asian team takes the field
variations: I couldn't name a single player, but that flag — that flag I recognize.
The comeback made me realize belonging needs no passport, just a heart that races along with theirs.

items.sourceNote: 英格蘭 4-2 克羅埃西亞 進球大戰；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
英格蘭四比二贏克羅埃西亞那場，六顆球進得我血壓跟著上上下下。
你以為要平淡收場，下一秒又有人破門。
看完我躺在沙發上喘氣，像剛跑完一場我根本沒下場的比賽。
這種場次提醒我：熬夜的風險很高，但中獎的時候，整夜的睡眠都值回票價。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個熬夜賭到一場進球大戰、隔天累但超滿足的朋友
variations: 六顆球的比賽，是世界盃發給熬夜球迷的頭獎。
英格蘭跟克羅埃西亞替我把整夜的睡眠，換成了一場不會忘記的比賽。

en.content:
England beat Croatia 4-2, and the six goals had my blood pressure riding up and down all night.
Just when you think it'll settle, someone smashes another one in.
I finished it sprawled on the couch, gasping, like I'd run a match I never actually played in.
Nights like this remind me: staying up is a gamble, but when it pays off, the whole night's sleep was worth the price.
en.editorNote: For the friend who gambled their sleep on a goal-fest and woke up wrecked but glowing
variations: A six-goal match is the jackpot the World Cup hands to night-owl fans.
England and Croatia turned my whole night of sleep into a match I'll never forget.

items.sourceNote: 蘇格蘭睽違 1998 後再進世界盃 首戰開胡；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
蘇格蘭睽違快三十年才再踢一次世界盃，首戰就拿下勝利。
我跟蘇格蘭毫無關係，卻看著新聞裡他們的球迷哭出來，自己鼻頭也酸了。
等了一輩子的事，終於發生在凌晨的這一刻。
世界盃會這樣偷襲你：用一支陌生球隊的眼淚，提醒你什麼叫值得等。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配深夜限動，獻給每個為遲到太久的好事掉過眼淚的人
variations: 等了二十八年的一場勝利，連旁觀的我都覺得時間沒有白費。
有些隊伍的勝利不靠實力動人，靠的是他們等了多久。

en.content:
Scotland made it back to the World Cup after nearly three decades and won their opener.
I have nothing to do with Scotland, yet watching their fans cry on screen, my own throat tightened.
A thing they'd waited a lifetime for, finally happening at this predawn hour.
The World Cup ambushes you like that: it uses a stranger's tears to remind you what it means to wait for something worth waiting for.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a late-night story post — for everyone who's cried over good news that took far too long
variations: A win twenty-eight years in the making — even as a bystander, I felt the wait was worth it.
Some teams' victories move you not through skill, but through how long they waited for them.

items.sourceNote: B 組四隊全平手各 1 分 完全膠著；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
B 組現在四隊全部一分，兩場都踢成一比一，整組卡在原地。
看著那張積分榜，我突然有種熟悉感——
這不就是我這週的進度條嗎？做了很多事，分數一動也不動。
小組賽教我的事：努力不一定有分差，但你至少還沒被淘汰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個這週超忙、成果卻看不太出來的同事，互相打氣一下
variations: 四隊全平手的積分榜，跟我的待辦清單一樣——看起來忙，其實原地踏步。
膠著不代表沒救，代表大家都還在桌上，下一場見真章。

en.content:
Group B is dead level — all four teams on one point, both games finished 1-1, the whole group stuck in place.
Looking at that table, I felt a strange familiarity:
isn't this just my progress bar this week? Did a ton of stuff, the number didn't budge.
What the group stage taught me: effort doesn't always show up as a gap, but at least you haven't been knocked out yet.
en.editorNote: For the coworker who was slammed all week with little to show for it — a little mutual encouragement
variations: A table with all four teams level looks just like my to-do list: looks busy, actually standing still.
A deadlock doesn't mean it's hopeless. It means everyone's still on the table, and the next match decides it.

items.sourceNote: 葡萄牙 1-1 剛果民主共和國 強隊被逼平；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
葡萄牙被剛果民主共和國逼成一比一的那場，
賽前我以為穩贏，結果弱隊死守到最後，硬是搶下一分。
那一分對葡萄牙是失望，對剛果卻是整個夏天最驕傲的夜晚。
世界盃最公平的地方：分數不問你是不是名門，只問你今晚拚了沒。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總是幫弱隊加油、看到強隊被逼平就偷笑的朋友
variations: 強隊掉的那一分，往往是弱隊一輩子的勳章。
名氣不會幫你進球，能逼平強隊的，從來是那九十分鐘的拚命。

en.content:
Portugal got held to a 1-1 draw by DR Congo.
I'd figured it was a sure win; instead the underdog defended to the death and snatched a point.
That one point was a disappointment for Portugal and the proudest night of the summer for Congo.
The fairest thing about the World Cup: the scoreline doesn't ask whether you're a powerhouse, only whether you fought tonight.
en.editorNote: For the friend who always backs the underdog and quietly grins when a giant gets held
variations: The point a giant drops is often a medal the underdog wears for life.
Reputation won't score for you. What holds a powerhouse to a draw is ninety minutes of sheer effort.

items.sourceNote: 維德角 0-0 逼平西班牙 弱隊死守一分；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
維德角零比零逼平西班牙的那一晚，全場沒進球，我卻看到不能呼吸。
一個人口比一座城市還少的國家，整整九十分鐘把西班牙擋在門外。
誰說零比零無聊？
那是一群名不見經傳的人，用一整夜的死守，換來他們這輩子最大聲的一分。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得零比零很悶、卻會為弱隊死守熱血起來的朋友
variations: 零比零也能讓我手心冒汗，前提是有一支弱隊在拚命守住底線。
西班牙丟的那兩分，是維德角守了九十分鐘才搶回來的尊嚴。

en.content:
The night Cape Verde held Spain to a 0-0 draw, nobody scored, and I still couldn't breathe.
A country smaller than most cities kept Spain out of the net for a full ninety minutes.
Who says 0-0 is boring?
That was a squad nobody had heard of trading a whole night of defending for the loudest single point of their lives.
en.editorNote: For the friend who finds 0-0 dull but gets fired up watching an underdog defend for its life
variations: A 0-0 can still make my palms sweat — as long as an underdog is fighting to hold the line.
The two points Spain dropped were the dignity Cape Verde defended for ninety minutes to take.

items.sourceNote: 加拿大 6-0 卡達 地主大勝；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
加拿大六比零屠了卡達，地主在自家球場踢得行雲流水。
我熬夜守在電視前，第三球進的時候還很興奮，
第六球進的時候，我已經邊鼓掌邊打哈欠。
大勝的比賽就是這樣：開頭是盛宴，結尾是你跟睡意的拉鋸戰。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個熬夜看大勝、看到後段開始跟瞌睡蟲奮戰的朋友
variations: 六比零的比賽，前半是慶典，後半是我跟眼皮的對決。
地主踢得越爽，我越清楚自己撐到底的真正對手是睡意。

en.content:
Canada crushed Qatar 6-0, the hosts gliding around their own pitch.
I stayed up glued to the screen, still buzzing when the third goal went in,
and by the sixth I was clapping and yawning at the same time.
That's how a rout goes: it starts as a feast and ends as a tug-of-war between you and your own sleepiness.
en.editorNote: For the friend who stays up for a blowout and ends up battling the urge to nod off
variations: A 6-0 match: the first half is a celebration, the second half is me versus my own eyelids.
The better the hosts play, the clearer it gets that my real opponent tonight is sleep.

items.sourceNote: 沙烏地阿拉伯 1-1 逼平兩屆冠軍烏拉圭；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
沙烏地阿拉伯一比一逼平拿過兩次冠軍的烏拉圭，賽前根本沒人看好他們。
那一分對沙烏地來說，比很多隊的勝利還珍貴。
我突然懂了一件事：
在世界盃，你不一定要贏過所有人，
有時候只要在最被低估的時候，證明自己沒那麼好欺負，就夠了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個常被低估、卻總在關鍵時刻證明自己的同路人
variations: 逼平兩屆冠軍的那一分，是沙烏地寫給所有看衰他們的人的回信。
不被看好沒關係，能在強隊面前站穩，本身就是一種勝利。

en.content:
Saudi Arabia held two-time champions Uruguay to a 1-1 draw — a team nobody had given a chance.
That single point meant more to the Saudis than many teams' wins.
And it hit me:
at the World Cup, you don't always have to beat everyone.
Sometimes, proving you're not as easy to push around as they assumed — exactly when you're most underestimated — is enough.
en.editorNote: For the comrade who gets underestimated but keeps proving themselves when it counts
variations: The point that held the two-time champions was Saudi Arabia's reply to everyone who wrote them off.
Being doubted is fine. Standing your ground against a giant is a victory in itself.

items.sourceNote: 開賽 23 場進 71 球 史上最高產世界盃；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
才踢了二十三場，就灌進七十一顆球，這屆是史上最會進球的世界盃。
別的好處不敢說，至少對熬夜的人很佛——
你撐到三點打開電視，看到進球的機率，比看到平淡的機率高多了。
這個夏天，連我失去的睡眠都進球進得很有效率。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個賭睡眠看球、卻常常賭中精彩場次的幸運朋友
variations: 七十一顆球分給二十三場，這屆世界盃對熬夜的人特別有誠意。
進球這麼多，我熬的每一個三點，幾乎都有回本。

en.content:
Just twenty-three matches in and there have already been seventy-one goals — the most prolific World Cup ever.
I can't promise much, but it's awfully kind to the night owls:
drag yourself up at 3 and turn on the TV, and your odds of catching a goal beat your odds of catching a snoozefest by a mile.
This summer, even the sleep I'm losing is being lost efficiently.
en.editorNote: For the lucky friend who gambles their sleep on a match and keeps landing on the good ones
variations: Seventy-one goals across twenty-three games — this World Cup is genuinely generous to the sleep-deprived.
With this many goals, almost every 3 AM I stay up for pays itself back.

items.sourceNote: 梅西 38 歲完成個人世界盃首頂帽子戲法 成史上最年長帽子戲法球員；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
梅西三十八歲了，這屆完成生涯世界盃第一次帽子戲法，還順手破了最年長紀錄。
三十八歲，很多人覺得該收一收了，他卻在凌晨的球場上連進三球。
我熬夜看的不只是進球，
是有人用一整個職業生涯告訴你：所謂太晚，常常只是別人替你下的結論。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老覺得自己「年紀大了來不及了」的朋友，讓他看看三十八歲能幹嘛
variations: 三十八歲的帽子戲法提醒我：別急著替自己宣布賽季結束。
別人問梅西還能踢多久，他用三顆球回答：能踢到我自己想停為止。

en.content:
Messi is thirty-eight, and this tournament he notched his first-ever World Cup hat trick — and casually became the oldest player to do it.
Thirty-eight, an age plenty of people think you should be winding down, and he's banging in three goals in a predawn match.
What I stayed up for wasn't just the goals,
it's someone using a whole career to tell you: 'too late' is usually just a verdict other people hand you.
en.editorNote: For the friend who keeps telling themselves they're 'too old, it's too late' — show them what thirty-eight can do
variations: A hat trick at thirty-eight reminds me: don't be in a rush to call your own season over.
People ask Messi how much longer he can play. He answered with three goals: as long as I decide to.

items.sourceNote: 加拿大 Jonathan David 上演帽子戲法 與梅西並列金靴榜首；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
金靴榜現在最前面有兩個名字：一個是梅西，一個是加拿大的 Jonathan David。
賽前所有人都在討論梅西、姆巴佩、哈蘭德，
結果一個沒什麼人提到的名字，也踢進三球擠到了最前面。
世界盃每年都在教同一件事：星光不會自動發光，敢上場、敢射門的人才會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總被忽略、卻默默把事情做到頂的同事或朋友
variations: 金靴榜不看你賽前多有名氣，只看你進了幾球。
梅西旁邊那個你不太熟的名字，才是這屆最值得記住的故事。

en.content:
There are two names at the top of the Golden Boot race right now: Messi, and Canada's Jonathan David.
Before the tournament everyone was talking Messi, Mbappé, Haaland —
and then a name barely anyone mentioned put away three goals and elbowed his way to the front.
The World Cup teaches the same lesson every year: the spotlight doesn't switch itself on. The people who show up and shoot turn it on.
en.editorNote: For the coworker or friend who always gets overlooked but quietly does the job better than anyone
variations: The Golden Boot race doesn't care how famous you were at kickoff. Only how many you scored.
That unfamiliar name next to Messi is the story of this tournament worth remembering.

items.sourceNote: 維德角 0-0 逼平西班牙 被稱本屆最慘輸盤；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
維德角逼平西班牙那場，被說是這屆下注輸最慘的一場。
那些押西班牙穩贏的人，整夜醒著，賠掉的不只是錢，還有對「紙上實力」的信心。
我沒下注，純粹熬夜看球，反而看得最爽。
世界盃提醒我：把希望全押在最被看好的那邊，往往是最容易心碎的那邊。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個習慣賭穩贏的那隊、卻一再被爆冷修理的朋友
variations: 賠最慘的不是押弱隊的人，是太相信強隊一定贏的人。
這場告訴我們：紙上的實力差距，在球場上隨時可以被一夜歸零。

en.content:
Cape Verde holding Spain to a 0-0 draw got called the biggest losing bet of the tournament so far.
Everyone who staked it all on a sure Spain win stayed up all night and lost more than money — they lost their faith in 'strength on paper.'
I didn't bet a cent, just stayed up to watch, and somehow had the best night of all.
The World Cup reminds me: putting all your hope on the heavy favorite is usually the surest way to get your heart broken.
en.editorNote: For the friend who always backs the safe favorite and keeps getting wrecked by upsets
variations: The biggest losers weren't the ones who backed the underdog. It was the ones who were sure the giant couldn't lose.
This match taught us: a gap in quality on paper can be wiped to zero in a single night on the pitch.

items.sourceNote: 哈蘭德世界盃處女秀梅開二度 助挪威多年後重返世界盃；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
哈蘭德這麼強，這居然是他第一次踢世界盃，處女秀就梅開二度。
挪威等他、也等這個舞台等了好多年。
再厲害的人，也得等到對的時間、對的舞台，才輪得到他發光。
所以如果你覺得自己一直還沒輪到，別灰心——有時候不是你不夠強，是舞台還沒搭好。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個能力很好、卻一直覺得「機會還沒來」的朋友
variations: 哈蘭德等了這麼久才站上世界盃，等到了就一次進兩球。
舞台會遲到，但只要你沒提前離場，總有輪到你發光的那天。

en.content:
Haaland is this good, and somehow this is his first ever World Cup — and he scored twice on his debut.
Norway waited years for him, and for this stage.
Even the most gifted people have to wait for the right time and the right stage before it's their turn to shine.
So if you feel like your turn still hasn't come, don't lose heart — sometimes it's not that you aren't good enough. It's that the stage isn't built yet.
en.editorNote: For the talented friend who keeps feeling like 'my chance hasn't arrived yet'
variations: Haaland waited this long to reach a World Cup, and when he got there he scored two on the spot.
The stage can run late, but as long as you don't leave early, your turn to shine will come.

items.sourceNote: 開賽 27 場進 88 球 史上最高產 熬夜球迷一週累積；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
才一個多禮拜，二十七場、八十八顆球，這屆進球多到誇張。
但對熬夜的人來說，累積的不只是進球數，
還有沒補回來的睡眠、喝掉的咖啡、和一張越來越下垂的臉。
世界盃進入第二週，我的身體和賽程表一樣——
上半場還精神抖擻，下半場全靠意志力硬撐。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個撐到第二週、體力開始見底卻死不放棄的戰友
variations: 進球數天天創新高，我的睡眠債也是。
第二週的球迷分兩種：已經放棄補眠的，和還在騙自己週末能補回來的。

en.content:
Just over a week in: twenty-seven matches, eighty-eight goals — an absurd haul.
But for the night owls, it's not only the goal count piling up,
it's the sleep we never recovered, the coffee we drained, and a face that sags a little more each day.
Week two of the World Cup, and my body is just like the schedule —
wide awake in the first half, running on pure willpower in the second.
en.editorNote: For the comrade who's made it to week two, running on empty but refusing to quit
variations: The goal count sets a new record daily. So does my sleep debt.
Week-two fans come in two kinds: the ones who've given up on catching up on sleep, and the ones still lying to themselves that the weekend will fix it.

items.sourceNote: 西班牙首輪被維德角逼和、第二輪 4-0 痛宰沙烏地阿拉伯強勢反彈；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
西班牙首輪被維德角逼平，全網笑了一整天。
第二輪他們四比零痛電沙烏地阿拉伯，像在跟全世界說：上一場當我沒踢。
看著看著我突然有感：
人生不怕第一場踢爛，怕的是踢爛之後就不敢再上場。
強隊的強，不是從不跌倒，是跌倒隔天就把場面贏回來。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個第一次搞砸後一直放不下、卻忘了自己還有下一場的朋友
variations: 被爆冷不可怕，被爆冷之後一蹶不振才可怕。
西班牙教我的事：第一輪的難看，要用第二輪的四比零來洗。

en.content:
Spain got held to a draw by Cape Verde in the first round, and the whole internet laughed for a day.
In the second round they hammered Saudi Arabia 4-0, as if telling the world: pretend that last game never happened.
Watching it, something hit me:
in life the danger isn't bombing your first game — it's never daring to step out for the next one.
What makes a great team great isn't never falling. It's winning it all back the very next day.
en.editorNote: For the friend still stuck on one mistake, forgetting they've got another match to play
variations: Getting upset isn't the scary part. Falling apart after the upset is.
What Spain taught me: you wash off an ugly first round with a 4-0 in the second.

items.sourceNote: 荷蘭 5-1 血洗瑞典 Brobbey 上半場梅開二度；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
荷蘭五比一血洗瑞典，上半場就進了一堆球。
我本來只想看個十分鐘就睡，
結果一球接一球，根本捨不得閉眼。
這種比賽最危險：你以為看完一球就去睡，
醒著的卻是整個凌晨，外加隔天那雙再也撐不開的眼皮。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個本來說看一下就睡、結果看完整場的朋友
variations: 進球太精彩的下場，就是你根本找不到去睡覺的時機。
五比一的比賽，每一球都在跟我的睡眠say goodbye。

en.content:
The Netherlands battered Sweden 5-1, piling up goals before halftime even arrived.
I only meant to watch ten minutes and then sleep.
But goal after goal kept coming, and I couldn't bring myself to shut my eyes.
This is the most dangerous kind of match: you tell yourself you'll sleep after one more goal,
and instead you stay up the whole predawn, plus a pair of eyelids that won't open the next day.
en.editorNote: For the friend who said they'd watch 'just a bit' and ended up seeing the whole match
variations: The price of goals this good is that you can never find a clean moment to go to bed.
A 5-1 match: every goal another goodbye to my sleep.

items.sourceNote: 日本 4-0 突尼西亞 創亞洲球隊世界盃最大勝差；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
日本四比零電爆突尼西亞，據說是亞洲球隊在世界盃的最大勝差。
我跟日本一點關係都沒有，
卻在凌晨握著拳頭，莫名替整個亞洲開心。
在這個別人家辦的派對裡，
看到鄰近的隊伍踢出名堂，總覺得我這一夜熬得特別有面子。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每逢亞洲球隊踢得好就莫名驕傲的朋友
variations: 我不是日本人，但亞洲球隊贏球的夜晚，我的黑眼圈也與有榮焉。
別人辦的世界盃，鄰桌踢得好，我也跟著抬頭挺胸。

en.content:
Japan crushed Tunisia 4-0, reportedly the biggest winning margin ever for an Asian side at a World Cup.
I have nothing to do with Japan,
yet there I was at 3 AM, fist clenched, inexplicably happy for the whole of Asia.
At this party someone else is throwing,
watching a team from my neighborhood pull off something special makes the lost sleep feel especially worth it.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels weirdly proud whenever an Asian team plays well
variations: I'm not Japanese, but on a night an Asian team wins, even my eye bags feel honored.
It's someone else's World Cup, but when the table next to mine plays well, I sit up a little straighter too.

items.sourceNote: 比利時被紅牌少打一人、與伊朗踢平 G 組形勢全亂；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
比利時下半場吃了一張紅牌，少打一人，硬是跟伊朗踢成平手，
整個小組形勢瞬間全亂。
我本來在算誰會出線，算到一半發現：
只要一張紅牌，我前面排的所有可能性全部重來。
世界盃跟人生一樣——你以為一切都安排好了，
總有一個你沒料到的意外，把整張計劃表重新洗牌。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個凡事都要先算好、卻常被一個意外打亂全盤的朋友
variations: 一張紅牌，毀掉的不只是比賽，還有我整晚的晉級計算。
計劃趕不上變化，變化趕不上一張第六十分鐘的紅牌。

en.content:
Belgium picked up a red card in the second half, played a man down, and still scraped a draw with Iran,
and just like that the whole group blew wide open.
I'd been working out who'd advance, and halfway through the math I realized:
one red card, and every possibility I'd lined up has to start over.
The World Cup is like life — you think it's all arranged,
and then one accident you never saw coming reshuffles the entire plan.
en.editorNote: For the friend who plans everything out and keeps getting derailed by one surprise
variations: One red card ruined more than the match. It ruined my whole night of advancement math.
Plans can't keep up with change, and change can't keep up with a red card in the 60th minute.

items.sourceNote: 本屆小組賽進球數爆量 連續大比分賽事；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆進球多到誇張，一場接一場大比分，
我每天都告訴自己「看完這場就睡」，
結果每天都因為下一場更精彩，又把鬧鐘往後撥一小時。
進球餵飽了我的眼睛，卻一口一口吃掉我的睡眠。
世界盃最甜蜜的陷阱就是：好看到讓你捨不得睡，睏到讓你撐不下去，你卻兩個都選了熬下去。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每天都說「看完就睡」、結果每天都食言的自己
variations: 進球越多，我越貪心，越貪心，我隔天越廢。
捨不得睡和睏到不行，這兩件事在世界盃期間，每天在我身上打架。

en.content:
The goals this year are absurd — one big scoreline after another.
Every day I tell myself 'I'll sleep after this match,'
and every day the next one looks even better, so I push the alarm back another hour.
The goals feast my eyes while quietly eating my sleep, bite by bite.
The World Cup's sweetest trap: too good to leave, too tired to stay, and you choose to push on through both anyway.
en.editorNote: For the version of you who says 'I'll sleep after this one' every day and breaks the promise every day
variations: The more goals there are, the greedier I get, and the more useless I am the next day.
Can't bear to sleep and can't keep my eyes open — during the World Cup, those two fight it out in me every single night.

items.sourceNote: C 羅 41 歲梅開二度 助葡萄牙 5-0 烏茲別克 成史上首位六屆世界盃都進球的球員；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
C 羅四十一歲了，這場梅開二度，幫葡萄牙五比零碾過烏茲別克，
還成了史上第一個連六屆世界盃都進球的人。
四十一歲耶，多少人這年紀早就在沙發上看別人踢，他卻還在凌晨的球場上開張。
我熬夜看的，從來不只是進球，
是有人用二十幾年告訴你：別人說你過氣的時候，正是你還站在場上的時候。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個老覺得自己「黃金時期過了」的朋友，讓他看看四十一歲還能進幾球
variations: 六屆世界盃都進球，C 羅證明的不是天賦，是不肯下場的執念。
四十一歲的梅開二度提醒我：過不過氣，不是別人說了算，是你還想不想踢。

en.content:
Ronaldo is forty-one, and this match he scored twice to power Portugal past Uzbekistan 5-0 —
becoming the first player ever to score at six different World Cups.
Forty-one. Plenty of people that age are on the couch watching others play, and he's out there opening his account in a predawn match.
What I stay up for was never just the goals,
it's someone spending twenty-odd years telling you: the moment they call you washed up is the moment you're still on the pitch.
en.editorNote: For the friend convinced their 'prime is behind them' — show them how many a forty-one-year-old can still score
variations: Scoring at six World Cups, Ronaldo proves it isn't talent. It's a refusal to leave the field.
A brace at forty-one reminds me: whether you're washed up isn't someone else's call. It's whether you still want to play.

items.sourceNote: 英格蘭 0-0 迦納 控球率高達 79% 卻零進球 近 60 年最高控球未進球紀錄；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
英格蘭控球率百分之七十九，整場壓著迦納打，最後零比零收場。
數據漂亮到不行，比分卻一動也不動。
我看著看著，突然在他們身上看到上班的自己——
忙了一整天，會開好開滿，訊息回好回滿，最後產出欄寫著：無。
世界盃這場提醒我：控球率不會幫你進球，把握機會的人才會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個整天看起來超忙、KPI 卻一格沒動的同事，互相療傷一下
variations: 百分之七十九的控球率配零進球，這不就是我那種「很忙但沒成果」的一天？
壓著對手打不算贏，把球放進網裡才算。忙跟有用，從來是兩回事。

en.content:
England had seventy-nine percent possession, pinned Ghana back all match, and it finished 0-0.
Gorgeous numbers, and a scoreline that wouldn't budge an inch.
Watching it, I suddenly saw my own workday in them —
busy all day, meetings packed, messages all answered, and under 'output' it just says: none.
This match reminded me: possession won't score for you. The one who takes the chance does.
en.editorNote: For the coworker who looks slammed all day while the KPI doesn't move a pixel — a little mutual healing
variations: Seventy-nine percent possession and zero goals — isn't that exactly my 'busy but nothing to show' kind of day?
Pinning your opponent back isn't winning. Putting the ball in the net is. Busy and useful were never the same thing.

items.sourceNote: 克羅埃西亞 1-0 巴拿馬、哥倫比亞 1-0 剛果民主共和國 同日兩場一球定生死；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
同一個凌晨，克羅埃西亞一比零贏巴拿馬，哥倫比亞也一比零小勝剛果。
一比零的比賽最折磨人：那唯一一顆球進了之後，剩下的時間全是煎熬，
生怕一個閃神就被追平，整夜的熬夜瞬間歸零。
我熬到最後一秒不是因為精彩，是因為我比場上的人還緊張。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個熬夜看一比零、看到補時還在屏住呼吸的朋友
variations: 一比零的後半段，比的不是球技，是誰的心臟比較強。
領先一球從來不是安心，是把整夜的睡眠都押在那條岌岌可危的底線上。

en.content:
Same predawn: Croatia beat Panama 1-0, and Colombia squeaked past DR Congo 1-0 too.
A 1-0 match is the most agonizing kind — once that single goal goes in, all the time left is pure torment,
terrified one lapse lets the equalizer through and turns the whole night of lost sleep to zero.
I stay up to the last second not because it's thrilling, but because I'm more nervous than the players on the pitch.
en.editorNote: For the friend who watches a 1-0 and is still holding their breath deep into stoppage time
variations: The back half of a 1-0 isn't a test of skill. It's a test of whose heart is stronger.
A one-goal lead is never reassurance. It's betting your whole night's sleep on a line that could break any second.

items.sourceNote: 48 隊新賽制 史上首次 32 隊晉級淘汰賽 含 8 個最佳第三名 晉級計算複雜；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
這屆史上第一次有三十二隊能晉級淘汰賽，連最好的八個第三名都算數。
小組末輪一到，我手機開了三個分頁，一邊算自家主隊的勝分，一邊算別組的恩怨情仇。
看球變成解數學題，而且還是有附帶條件的那種。
但你知道嗎，正因為連第三名都有活路，這個夜晚才沒有一支球隊願意提早放棄。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個小組末輪邊看球邊瘋狂算晉級組合、算到頭痛的朋友
variations: 三十二隊晉級的好處：到最後一輪，幾乎沒有一場是沒意義的垃圾時間。
連第三名都留一條路，等於告訴每支球隊——只要還沒被淘汰，就值得再拚一場。

en.content:
For the first time ever, thirty-two teams advance to the knockouts this year — even the eight best third-placed sides count.
The instant the final round of group games starts, I've got three browser tabs open, working out my team's points on one and other groups' grudges on another.
Watching football turns into solving a math problem — the kind with conditions attached.
But here's the thing: precisely because even third place has a way through, not a single team is willing to give up early tonight.
en.editorNote: For the friend who spends the final group round watching while frantically running advancement scenarios until their head hurts
variations: The upside of thirty-two teams advancing: in the final round, almost no match is meaningless garbage time.
Leaving a path open even for third place tells every team: as long as you're not eliminated, it's worth fighting one more match.

items.sourceNote: 同一週梅西帽子戲法、C 羅梅開二度 兩大傳奇高齡同台 GOAT 之爭再起；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
同一個禮拜，梅西上演帽子戲法，C 羅梅開二度，
兩個快四十、被講了一輩子「誰才是最強」的人，又同時在球場上開火。
我從小看他們踢到現在，這屆很可能是最後一次在世界盃同框。
所以我熬的不只是這幾場球，是一個時代的尾巴。
等他們真的退了，凌晨三點就再也找不到這麼值得爬起來的理由了。
zh-tw.editorNote: 適合配深夜限動，獻給每個從學生時代看梅西 C 羅看到現在出社會的球迷
variations: 誰是 GOAT 我沒答案，但能同時看到他們兩個還在進球，本身就是我這代人的福氣。
這屆過後，凌晨三點的電視裡，可能就再也沒有他們兩個的名字了——所以這幾夜我一場都不想缺席。

en.content:
In the same week, Messi pulled off a hat trick and Ronaldo scored a brace —
two men pushing forty, argued over their whole lives as to which is the greatest, firing away on the pitch at the very same time.
I've watched them play since I was a kid, and this is very likely the last World Cup they'll share.
So what I'm staying up for isn't just these few matches. It's the tail end of an era.
Once they really retire, 3 AM will never again hand me a reason this good to drag myself out of bed.
en.editorNote: Perfect for a late-night story post — for every fan who's watched Messi and Ronaldo since their school days and is now out in the working world
variations: I don't have an answer for who's the GOAT, but getting to watch both of them still scoring at once is, in itself, my generation's good fortune.
After this tournament, their two names may never share a 3 AM broadcast again — so I don't want to miss a single one of these nights.

items.sourceNote: 台灣時間 6/28 小組賽 12 組全數收官 32 強名單底定；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
今天小組賽十二組全部踢完，三十二強名單終於底定。
從開幕熬到現在，半個多月，我的作息已經徹底投降。
但奇怪的是，看到「小組賽結束」這四個字，我居然有點捨不得。
那些誰都不認識的弱隊、那些零比零的悶戰，原來也悄悄陪我度過了好多個凌晨。
淘汰賽要開始了，從現在起，輸一場就回家——包括我那條早就回不去的作息。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個跟你一起從開幕熬到小組賽收官、作息全毀的戰友
variations: 小組賽結束的這一刻才發現，陪我熬過最多夜的，其實是那些根本沒人看好的隊伍。
從四十八隊熬到三十二強，淘汰的不只是球隊，還有我所剩無幾的正常作息。

en.content:
Today all twelve groups wrapped up, and the round of 32 is finally set.
From the opener to now — over two weeks — my sleep schedule has completely surrendered.
But oddly, seeing the words 'group stage over,' I felt a pang of not wanting it to end.
Those nobody teams, those goalless slogs — turns out they quietly kept me company through so many predawns.
The knockouts begin now: from here on, lose once and you go home. Including my sleep schedule, which left long ago.
en.editorNote: For the comrade who stayed up with you from the opener to the final group whistle, sleep schedule in ruins
variations: Only when the group stage ended did I realize the teams that kept me up most were the ones nobody believed in.
Grinding from forty-eight teams down to thirty-two, what got eliminated wasn't just clubs. It's whatever normal sleep I had left.

items.sourceNote: 8 個最佳第三名出爐 塞內加爾以唯一 3 分入選；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
八個最佳第三名出爐，其中有一隊只拿三分，硬是擠進淘汰賽。
別組第三名早就收拾行李回家，他們卻靠著一點點得失球差，活了下來。
我看著那條晉級線，突然覺得很勵志——
原來不是非得贏到第一，才有資格繼續走下去。
有時候你只是表現得沒那麼差，命運就願意再給你一場的機會。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個覺得自己不夠頂尖、快要放棄的朋友，第三名也能晉級啊
variations: 靠三分晉級的隊伍提醒我：活下來不一定要最強，有時候只要比別人少輸一點。
最好的八個第三名告訴我一件事——不是只有冠軍才配擁有下一場。

en.content:
The eight best third-placed teams are in, and one of them squeaked through on just three points.
Other groups' third-place sides already packed up and went home; this one survived on a sliver of goal difference.
Looking at that qualifying line, I found it strangely inspiring —
turns out you don't have to finish first to earn the right to keep going.
Sometimes you just have to be a little less bad than the rest, and fate hands you one more match.
en.editorNote: For the friend who feels they're not elite enough and is about to give up — third place advances too, you know
variations: A team advancing on three points reminds me: surviving doesn't require being the strongest, just losing a little less than everyone else.
The eight best third-placed sides taught me one thing — the next match isn't reserved only for champions.

items.sourceNote: 32 強淘汰賽 6/28 南非 vs 加拿大揭幕（地主對非洲隊）；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
淘汰賽第一場，地主加拿大對上南非。
從今天起，比賽再也沒有「下一輪再扳回來」這種事——
九十分鐘踢完，輸的那隊，整個夏天就到此為止。
小組賽我可以邊看邊滑手機，淘汰賽我連去倒杯水都不敢。
世界盃進入下半場，每一場都像在看別人的人生，被一顆球決定。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個小組賽很佛、一進淘汰賽就開始手心冒汗的朋友
variations: 淘汰賽的殘酷在於：沒有明天，只有這九十分鐘，外加可能的延長與十二碼。
從今晚開始，我看的不是比賽，是一群人會不會在凌晨被一顆球送回家。

en.content:
First knockout match: host Canada against South Africa.
From today, there's no more 'we'll make it up next round' —
the ninety minutes end, and for the losing side, the whole summer is over right there.
In the group stage I could watch and scroll my phone at the same time. In the knockouts I don't even dare get up for water.
The World Cup hits its back half, and every match feels like watching someone's life decided by a single ball.
en.editorNote: For the friend who was relaxed in the group stage and starts sweating the moment the knockouts begin
variations: The cruelty of the knockouts: no tomorrow, just these ninety minutes — plus possible extra time and penalties.
From tonight I'm not watching matches. I'm watching whether a group of people gets sent home at 3 AM by one ball.

items.sourceNote: 法國 4-1 大勝挪威 末輪火力全開；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
法國末輪四比一痛電挪威，火力全開，像在提醒大家別忘了他們的位置。
小組賽前兩場有些強隊都還在熱身、藏實力，
等到攸關晉級的最後一場，才一次把油門踩到底。
看著看著我突然懂了——真正的強者，不會每一場都用盡全力，
他們只是把最狠的那一手，留到非贏不可的那個夜晚。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個平常看起來很佛、關鍵時刻才全力爆發的朋友
variations: 強隊藏實力藏到末輪才四比一爆發，這種人最可怕——你永遠不知道他底牌還有多深。
不是每場都拚命才叫厲害，懂得把全力留到對的那一夜，才是真本事。

en.content:
France hammered Norway 4-1 in the final group match, firing on all cylinders, as if to remind everyone where they stand.
In the first two group games some big sides were still warming up, holding back their hand,
then, in the match that decided advancement, they finally floored it.
Watching it, I suddenly understood: real heavyweights don't give their all every single match.
They just save their fiercest hand for the night they absolutely have to win.
en.editorNote: For the friend who looks laid-back most of the time but unleashes everything when it counts
variations: A favorite holding back until a 4-1 explosion in the final round — that's the scariest kind. You never know how deep their hand really goes.
Going all out every match isn't what makes you great. Knowing to save it all for the right night is the real skill.

items.sourceNote: 32 強對戰組合公布 球迷瘋狂排路徑推演到決賽；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
三十二強對戰表一公布，我立刻開始幹一件最沒意義的事——
沿著我主隊的那條線，一路推演，假設每一場都贏，直接腦補到決賽。
小組賽都還沒消化完，我已經在想冠軍獎盃要怎麼舉了。
理智上我知道，這條線九成九會在某一場斷掉。
但對戰表剛出爐的這一夜，每支球隊的球迷都還活在那個還沒輸的、最美好的版本裡。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個對戰表一出來就開始幫主隊腦補奪冠路線的朋友
variations: 對戰表最快樂的時刻，是它剛公布、還沒開踢，每個人的主隊都還沒輸的那一夜。
我沿著那條線一路腦補到決賽，明知是幻覺，還是捨不得醒。

en.content:
The moment the round-of-32 bracket dropped, I started doing the most pointless thing imaginable —
tracing my team's line all the way through, assuming we win every match, fast-forwarding straight to the final.
I haven't even digested the group stage and I'm already picturing how the trophy gets lifted.
Rationally I know this line will almost certainly snap somewhere along the way.
But on the night the bracket first comes out, every team's fans are still living in the most beautiful version — the one where they haven't lost yet.
en.editorNote: For the friend who maps out their team's entire path to the title the second the bracket is released
variations: The happiest moment of a bracket is right after it drops, before a ball is kicked, the night nobody's team has lost yet.
I traced that line all the way to the final, knowing it's a fantasy, and still couldn't bear to wake up.

items.sourceNote: 巴拉圭 PK 4-3 爆冷淘汰德國，門將撲兩球；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
巴拉圭跟德國踢到 PK，我熬到那一刻已經快天亮。
一顆一顆罰，每一球我的心臟都跟著停一下。
門將撲出兩球的瞬間，連我這個賽前根本沒押巴拉圭的人，都從沙發上彈起來。
PK 大戰最折磨的地方在於：踢了一百二十分鐘，最後卻要用一種跟比賽幾乎無關的方式分勝負。
但也正因為這樣，那個爆冷的夜晚，才會一輩子記得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個陪你熬到 PK、最後一起為冷門隊尖叫的朋友
variations: PK 大戰的每一球，都是一次心臟被捏住又放開的過程。
踢一百二十分鐘分不出勝負，最後一顆十二碼，把整夜的等待一次兌現。

en.content:
Paraguay and Germany went to penalties, and by then I'd stayed up almost to dawn.
One kick at a time, my heart stopping a beat with each one.
The instant the keeper saved his second, even I — who never bet on Paraguay — shot up off the couch.
The torture of a shootout is this: you play a hundred and twenty minutes, then decide it all with something that barely resembles the game.
But that's exactly why a night like that, the upset, stays with you forever.
en.editorNote: For the friend who stayed up with you to the shootout and screamed for the underdog at the end
variations: Every kick in a shootout is your heart being squeezed and released all over again.
A hundred and twenty minutes settle nothing, then one penalty cashes in a whole night of waiting.

items.sourceNote: 摩洛哥補時絕平、PK 3-2 淘汰荷蘭；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
荷蘭眼看就要贏了，摩洛哥在補時第一分鐘硬是扳平。
那一球進的時候，我跟著全世界倒抽一口氣——
本來以為可以去睡了，結果延長賽加 PK，又被多綁架了半小時。
世界盃教我的事：比賽沒結束之前，永遠不要先去刷牙準備睡覺。
你以為的終點，常常只是別人逆轉的起點。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個提早關電視、結果錯過絕殺的朋友，叫他下次撐到哨音響
variations: 補時絕平的那一秒，是把所有準備睡覺的人重新抓回戰場。
領先不等於贏，哨音響之前，沒有任何一場比賽是安全的。

en.content:
The Netherlands looked like winners, then Morocco forced an equalizer in the first minute of stoppage time.
When that goal went in, I gasped along with the rest of the world —
I'd been ready to go to bed, and now extra time plus penalties kidnapped me for another half hour.
What the World Cup taught me: never go brush your teeth for bed before the match is actually over.
The finish line you assumed is often just the starting line for someone else's comeback.
en.editorNote: For the friend who turned off the TV early and missed the winner — tell them to hold out for the whistle next time
variations: A stoppage-time equalizer drags everyone who was heading to bed right back to the battlefield.
A lead isn't a win. Until the whistle, no match is ever safe.

items.sourceNote: 巴西補時 Martinelli 絕殺 2-1 淘汰日本；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
日本先進球，巴西追平，眼看就要拖進延長賽，
結果補時一記絕殺，巴西二比一把日本送回家。
那顆球進的時候，我一個跟兩隊都沒關係的人，居然替日本覺得心痛。
補時絕殺最殘忍的地方在於：
你撐了快九十分鐘，命運卻在最後一分鐘，連反應的時間都不給你。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個替輸球的隊伍難過、自己卻沒在那個國家住過的朋友
variations: 補時絕殺是世界盃最快樂也最殘忍的一秒，端看你站在哪一邊。
九十分鐘都撐過來了，卻倒在最後一分鐘，這就是淘汰賽。

en.content:
Japan scored first, Brazil drew level, and it looked headed to extra time —
then a stoppage-time winner sent Japan home, 2-1 to Brazil.
When that goal went in, I — with no stake in either side — actually ached for Japan.
The cruelty of a last-gasp winner is this:
you hold on for nearly ninety minutes, and fate strikes in the final one, leaving you no time even to react.
en.editorNote: For the friend who grieves for the losing team despite never having lived in that country
variations: A stoppage-time winner is the World Cup's happiest and cruelest second, depending on which side you're on.
You survived ninety minutes only to fall in the last one. That's the knockout stage.

items.sourceNote: 地主加拿大第 92 分鐘 Eustáquio 絕殺 1-0 南非；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
地主加拿大跟南非僵持到最後，第九十二分鐘才靠一顆球絕殺，一比零晉級。
看到第八十分鐘還零比零的時候，我已經在心裡盤算等等要直接去睡，
結果就差那兩分鐘，全場炸開，我的睡意也跟著飛了。
淘汰賽最迷人的，就是這種「再撐一下下」的執念——
你永遠不知道，那顆改變一切的球，會不會就在你準備放棄的下一秒進。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總在最後關頭差點放棄、卻又咬牙撐住的朋友
variations: 第九十二分鐘的絕殺，獎勵的是所有沒提早關電視的人。
再撐一下下，常常就是淘汰賽留給堅持者的禮物。

en.content:
Host Canada and South Africa stayed deadlocked until the very end, then a 92nd-minute strike sealed it, 1-0, and through they went.
At the 80th minute it was still 0-0, and I'd already started planning to just go to bed.
Then, in those last two minutes, the whole place erupted, and my sleepiness vanished with it.
The most captivating thing about the knockouts is that 'just a little longer' compulsion —
you never know if the ball that changes everything goes in the very second after you'd have given up.
en.editorNote: For the friend who almost quits at the last moment but always grits their teeth and holds on
variations: A 92nd-minute winner rewards everyone who didn't turn off the TV early.
Just a little longer is often the gift the knockouts save for whoever holds on.

items.sourceNote: 32 強前四場：兩場 PK 加多記絕殺 全程精彩；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
三十二強才開打四場，就塞了兩場 PK 大戰加好幾顆補時絕殺。
我本來只打算看一場就去睡，結果一場接一場，根本捨不得關。
淘汰賽最可怕的不是凌晨開賽，是它一場比一場精彩，
讓你每次都告訴自己「看完這場就睡」，然後天就亮了。
這個夏天我欠的不是睡眠，是當初那句「再看一場」。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個每晚都說「看完這場就睡」、結果看到天亮的朋友
variations: 「看完這場就睡」是世界盃期間最大的謊言，而且我每天都說。
淘汰賽不需要逼你熬夜，它只要夠精彩，你就自己捨不得去睡。

en.content:
Only four matches into the round of 32 and we'd already had two penalty shootouts plus a handful of stoppage-time winners.
I'd planned to watch one and go to sleep, but one game rolled into the next and I couldn't bring myself to switch it off.
The scary thing about the knockouts isn't the predawn kickoffs — it's that each match outdoes the last,
so every time you tell yourself 'just this one, then bed,' and then the sun comes up.
What I owe this summer isn't sleep. It's every 'just one more match' I ever said.
en.editorNote: For the friend who says 'just this match, then bed' every night and ends up watching till sunrise
variations: 'Just this match, then bed' is the biggest lie of the World Cup, and I tell it daily.
The knockouts don't have to force you to stay up. They just have to be good enough that you can't bear to sleep.

items.sourceNote: 地主美國十人應戰仍 2-0 勝，睽違 2002 首度贏得淘汰賽；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
地主美國少一個人踢，還是二比零守到最後，
拿下他們睽違二十幾年、二○○二年之後的第一場淘汰賽勝利。
十個人對十一個人，多累啊，可是他們就是咬著牙撐完。
凌晨看到這種比賽我特別有共鳴——
因為我上班也常常是一個人做兩個人的事，只是我沒有全場替我加油而已。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個團隊臨時少一個人、卻還是硬把案子做完的同事
variations: 少一個人還能贏，靠的不是天分，是全隊一起多扛一點。
十人應戰的比賽最好看，因為每個人都在做超出自己份內的事。

en.content:
The host U.S. played a man down and still kept it 2-0 to the whistle,
taking their first knockout win since 2002, a wait of over twenty years.
Ten against eleven — how exhausting — and they just gritted their teeth and saw it through.
Watching a game like that at 3 AM hits home for me,
because at work I'm often one person doing two people's jobs too, minus a stadium cheering me on.
en.editorNote: For the coworker who lost a teammate at the last minute and still forced the project over the line
variations: Winning a man down isn't about talent. It's everyone agreeing to carry a little more.
A ten-man match is the best kind to watch, because everyone's doing more than their share.

items.sourceNote: Balogun 進球後遭紅牌，事後認定 VAR 程序誤判、下場禁賽；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
巴洛根上半場才進球，下半場就被一張紅牌請下場，
結果事後檢討，說 VAR 那次程序根本判錯了。
判都判了，人也罰下去了，下一場還照樣停賽。
凌晨三點看到這種畫面最無力——
明明是別人搞錯，最後買單、扛後果的，還是最認真的那一個。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明明沒錯、卻被流程判出局，隔天還要收拾殘局的朋友
variations: 系統判錯，人受罰，這種劇情不只發生在球場上。
最委屈的不是輸球，是明明沒犯規，卻要替別人的誤判停賽一場。

en.content:
Balogun scored in the first half, then got shown a red in the second and marched off,
and afterward the review said the VAR call had followed the wrong protocol entirely.
But the card stood, the man was gone, and the suspension for the next match still counts.
Seeing that at 3 AM is the most helpless feeling —
someone else got it wrong, and the one who pays for it is still the guy who tried hardest.
en.editorNote: For the friend who did nothing wrong but got ruled out by process and still has to clean up the mess the next day
variations: The system errs, the person pays. That plot isn't confined to a football pitch.
The real injustice isn't losing. It's being suspended a game for a foul that review says you never committed.

items.sourceNote: 比利時終場前連追兩球逼平、延長賽 3-2 逆轉塞內加爾；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
塞內加爾眼看要贏了，比利時卻在終場前連進兩球把比分追平，
再拖進延長賽，最後三比二硬是逆轉。
看到快落後的時候我都已經打哈欠了，
結果那兩顆追平球，直接把我從半夢半醒拉回沙發正中央。
世界盃教會我最重要的一件事：只要哨音還沒響，你以為的結局都不算數。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個總在最後一刻才逆轉、害你不敢提早去睡的朋友
variations: 終場前連追兩球，是把所有準備關電視的人重新綁回沙發。
落後不等於輸，這句話比利時用兩顆補時的球親自示範給我看。

en.content:
Senegal looked like winners, then Belgium struck twice before the whistle to level it,
dragged it into extra time, and clawed back a 3-2 comeback.
I was already yawning when they went behind,
and those two equalizers yanked me straight from half-asleep back to the middle of the couch.
The biggest thing the World Cup taught me: until the whistle blows, the ending you assumed doesn't count.
en.editorNote: For the friend whose comebacks always come at the death, keeping you from daring to sleep early
variations: Two late goals to level it drags everyone reaching for the remote right back onto the couch.
Behind isn't beaten. Belgium demonstrated that to me personally, twice, in stoppage time.

items.sourceNote: 英格蘭 2-1 勝剛果民主共和國，上半場一度落後；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
英格蘭這種等級的隊，賽前我以為穩贏，
結果剛果先進一球，上半場還讓英格蘭落後著進休息室。
我一個沒押任何一隊的人，看得比誰都緊張。
最後英格蘭二比一翻回來，我才發現：
世界盃裡沒有一場是穩的，越被看好的隊，越可能在凌晨三點嚇你一跳。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個賽前信心滿滿押強隊、上半場開始冒冷汗的朋友
variations: 紙上排名越漂亮的隊，越容易在上半場給你一記悶棍。
以為穩贏的比賽，往往才是讓你熬到最緊張的那一場。

en.content:
A team England's size — before kickoff I figured it was a lock.
Then DR Congo scored first and sent England into the break trailing.
With no stake in either side, I was somehow more nervous than anyone.
England flipped it back to 2-1 in the end, and only then did it hit me:
no match at the World Cup is ever safe. The bigger the favorite, the more likely they scare you half to death at 3 AM.
en.editorNote: For the friend who backed the favorite full of confidence and started sweating by halftime
variations: The prettier a team looks on paper, the more likely they clock you in the first half.
The match you assumed was a lock is often the one that keeps you up the most tense.

items.sourceNote: 32 強落幕、16 強對戰出爐（加拿大 vs 摩洛哥、巴拉圭 vs 法國等）；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
三十二強打完，十六強的對戰表剛出爐，
加拿大碰摩洛哥、巴拉圭抽到法國，一場比一場更難睡。
最現實的是，隊伍越少，代表能熬夜的日子也越少了。
看著剩下的名單，我有種矛盾的心情——
既希望它一場一場踢下去，又捨不得這個為別人的國家失眠的夏天，離結束又近了一步。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個一邊排下輪熬夜行程、一邊捨不得世界盃快結束的朋友
variations: 十六強對戰表出爐的那一刻，是興奮和不捨同時報到。
隊伍越剩越少，我能名正言順熬夜的夜晚，也越剩越少。

en.content:
The round of 32 is done and the round-of-16 bracket just dropped —
Canada drew Morocco, Paraguay pulled France, each tie harder to sleep through than the last.
The hard truth is, fewer teams left means fewer nights left to stay up.
Looking at what remains, I feel two things at once:
I want it to keep going match by match, and I can't bear that this summer of losing sleep for other people's countries is one step closer to ending.
en.editorNote: For the friend planning their next round of late nights while mourning that the World Cup is nearly over
variations: The moment the round-of-16 bracket drops, excitement and heartache clock in together.
The fewer teams left, the fewer nights I get to stay up with a good excuse.

items.sourceNote: 巴拉圭 PK 4-3 淘汰德國，本屆最大爆冷，德國連三屆止步；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
世界排名第十的德國，被排名四十幾的巴拉圭，用一場 PK 大戰送回家。
三個罰球射失，一整支傳統強隊，就這樣在十二碼前面崩塌。
我熬夜熬到 PK 才發現一件事：
紙上的排名、過去的光環，在那顆定生死的球面前，全部歸零。
世界盃不看你以前多強，只看你這一腳敢不敢。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個押了德國、結果被巴拉圭爆冷氣到睡不著的朋友
variations: 排名第十輸給第四十幾，這種夜晚才是世界盃存在的意義。
十二碼前面沒有強隊弱隊，只有敢踢和不敢踢的人。

en.content:
Germany, ranked tenth in the world, got sent home by Paraguay, ranked somewhere in the forties, in a penalty shootout.
Three missed spot-kicks, and an entire traditional powerhouse just crumbled at twelve yards.
I stayed up all the way to the shootout and it finally hit me:
the ranking on paper, the glory of the past — none of it survives that one decisive kick.
The World Cup doesn't care how strong you used to be. It only asks whether you dare take this one.
en.editorNote: For the friend who backed Germany and couldn't sleep after Paraguay pulled off the upset
variations: Tenth in the world losing to forty-something — that kind of night is the whole reason the World Cup exists.
At twelve yards there are no favorites and no minnows, only those who dare to shoot and those who don't.

items.sourceNote: 比利時對塞內加爾，延長賽末段 VAR 審七分鐘後判爭議 PK，蒂勒曼斯罰進 3-2 淘汰塞內加爾；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
延長賽最後幾分鐘，裁判被 VAR 叫去看螢幕，一看就是七分鐘。
凌晨三點的我，那七分鐘比整場九十分鐘還漫長。
最後判了一個連對手球迷都覺得牽強的 PK，比利時就這樣三比二絕殺塞內加爾。
看完只想說一句：
熬了一整夜，最怕的不是輸，是輸得讓你連生氣都不知道該對誰生氣。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個陪你熬夜、最後被一個爭議判決搞到一起罵螢幕的朋友
variations: VAR 看七分鐘，我在沙發上老了七歲。
最難接受的出局，不是踢輸的，是被一個看半天還看不清楚的判決判掉的。

en.content:
In the dying minutes of extra time, the ref got called to the screen — and stared at it for seven whole minutes.
At 3 AM, those seven minutes felt longer than the entire ninety before them.
He gave a penalty even the opposing fans found a stretch, and Belgium killed Senegal off 3-2.
All I could think afterward was this:
after a whole night up, the worst part isn't losing — it's losing in a way where you don't even know who to be angry at.
en.editorNote: For the friend who stayed up with you and ended up yelling at the screen over one controversial call
variations: VAR looked at it for seven minutes; I aged seven years on the couch.
The hardest exit to accept isn't being outplayed — it's being knocked out by a call nobody could see clearly even after staring at it.

items.sourceNote: 塞內加爾一度 2-0 領先卻遭比利時逆轉出局；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
塞內加爾一度二比零領先，我都準備幫他們慶祝晉級了。
結果比利時一球一球追上來，最後把他們逆轉出局。
兩球領先，聽起來很安全，卻是世界盃裡最危險的比分。
那一晚我學到的不只是足球——
領先的時候別急著鬆懈，因為真正的比賽，往往是你以為快贏的那一刻才開始。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個做事總在領先時鬆懈、最後被反超的朋友，順便提醒你自己
variations: 二比零是全世界最會騙人的比分，塞內加爾用一整晚幫我證明。
領先不等於贏，這句話凌晨三點在我沙發上重播了一遍。

en.content:
Senegal were up 2-0, and I was already getting ready to celebrate them through.
Then Belgium clawed it back goal by goal and knocked them out in the end.
A two-goal lead sounds safe, but it's the most dangerous scoreline at the World Cup.
What I learned that night wasn't just about football —
don't ease off when you're ahead, because the real game often starts the very moment you think you've won.
en.editorNote: For the friend who always relaxes once they're ahead and gets overtaken at the end — and a reminder to yourself too
variations: 2-0 is the most deceptive scoreline on earth, and Senegal spent a whole night proving it to me.
Ahead isn't won — a lesson that replayed on my couch at 3 AM.

items.sourceNote: 16 強 7/3 開打，阿根廷、澳洲等隊出賽；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
三十二強打完，本來以為可以喘口氣、補一下這幾週欠的睡眠。
結果十六強今天就開打，阿根廷、澳洲一場接一場排回凌晨。
我的身體剛想切回正常作息，賽程表又把它一把拉回深夜。
世界盃期間的補眠，從來只是幻覺——
它給你喘息，只是為了讓你下一場熬得更甘願。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個以為淘汰賽之間可以睡飽、結果馬上又要熬夜的朋友
variations: 剛想補眠，十六強就開打，這種節奏是世界盃專屬的酷刑。
小組賽到淘汰賽之間的空檔，短到我連生理時鐘都來不及重開機。

en.content:
The round of 32 wrapped up and I figured I could finally breathe and repay a few weeks of sleep debt.
Then the round of 16 kicked off today — Argentina, Australia, match after match sliding right back into the small hours.
My body was just about to switch back to a normal schedule, and the fixture list yanked it straight back into the night.
Catching up on sleep during the World Cup was always an illusion —
it gives you a breather only to make you gladly stay up for the next one.
en.editorNote: For the friend who thought they'd sleep in between knockout rounds and immediately had to pull another all-nighter
variations: Just as I reached for some sleep, the round of 16 kicked off — a torture unique to the World Cup.
The gap between the group stage and the knockouts is too short for even my body clock to reboot.

items.sourceNote: PK 大戰罰球員承受的壓力，一腳定生死；改寫原創

zh-tw.content:
PK 大戰的時候，我坐在沙發上手心都在冒汗，
可我想的是那個要站上十二碼的球員：
全世界幾億人盯著，四年的努力，濃縮成走向罰球點那短短幾步。
凌晨三點我終於懂了——
真正的勇敢，不是不害怕，是害怕到極點，還願意把球放上去，往前踏一步。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個正站在人生某個十二碼前、猶豫要不要往前踏的朋友
variations: 敢站上罰球點的人，都已經贏了一半，剩下的交給運氣。
四年的努力壓在一腳，這種壓力我在辦公室從沒扛過，卻在凌晨三點替別人扛了一次。

en.content:
During the shootout I sat on the couch with my palms sweating,
but what I was really thinking about was the player who had to step up to the spot:
hundreds of millions watching, four years of work compressed into those few short steps toward the penalty mark.
At 3 AM it finally made sense to me —
real courage isn't the absence of fear. It's being terrified out of your mind and still setting the ball down and stepping forward.
en.editorNote: For the friend standing at some twelve-yard spot in their own life, hesitating over whether to step up
variations: Anyone who dares to walk up to the spot has already won half of it; the rest is down to luck.
Four years of work riding on a single kick — a pressure I've never carried at the office, yet carried for a stranger at 3 AM.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content:
九十分鐘平手，主播說要打加時，
我看了一眼牆上的時鐘：凌晨兩點四十。
再三十分鐘，還可能有 PK。
那一刻我做了一個成年人最艱難的決定——
不是關電視去睡，而是打開行事曆，把明天早上的會議往後挪一小時。
有些球賽值得你用一整個上午的精神去換，這場就是。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個明知道要早起、卻還是決定看完加時的球友
variations: 加時賽不是延長痛苦，是延長浪漫，只是浪漫的代價由明天的我來付。
九十分鐘沒分勝負，那就再給我三十分鐘的失眠，我甘願。

en.content:
Ninety minutes, still tied, and the commentator says we're going to extra time.
I glanced at the clock on the wall: 2:40 AM.
Thirty more minutes, and maybe penalties after that.
That's when I made the hardest decision an adult can make —
not to turn off the TV and sleep, but to open my calendar and push tomorrow's meeting back an hour.
Some matches are worth trading a whole morning of focus for. This was one of them.
en.editorNote: For the friend who knew they had an early start and stayed for extra time anyway
variations: Extra time isn't extended suffering, it's extended romance — I just leave the bill for tomorrow-me to pay.
Ninety minutes settled nothing, so give me thirty more minutes of insomnia. Gladly.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content:
小組賽最後一輪，我支持的隊要贏球，還要等另一場的結果幫忙。
我一邊看比賽，一邊在手機上刷另一場的比分，兩隻眼睛分給兩座球場。
最後那顆進球出現的時候，隔壁桌不認識的人跟我同時站起來大叫。
那一秒我突然明白，熬夜看球熬的從來不是輸贏，
是那種「還有機會、還沒結束」的心跳，在生活裡越來越難得。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個還在等一個結果、還沒放棄的人
variations: 還沒被淘汰，就還有故事可以寫，人生和小組賽都一樣。
把兩隻眼睛分給兩座球場的那一晚，是我今年最專心的一次。

en.content:
Last round of the group stage: my team needed to win, and needed a favor from the other match too.
I watched one game while refreshing the other score on my phone — one eye for each stadium.
When that final goal finally went in, a stranger at the next table and I stood up and yelled at the exact same second.
And it hit me that staying up for soccer was never really about winning or losing.
It's that feeling of 'there's still a chance, it's not over yet' — something life gives you less and less.
en.editorNote: For the person still waiting on a result, still not giving up
variations: As long as you're not eliminated, there's still a story left to write — group stages and life both work that way.
The night I split my two eyes between two stadiums was the most focused I've been all year.

items.sourceNote: internal only

zh-tw.content:
我支持的不是自己國家的隊，是十幾年前一場比賽偶然愛上的隊。
沒有人陪我熬夜，公司群組沒人討論，贏了也沒人跟我擊掌。
凌晨三點，客廳只有我和電視的光。
可是每次他們進球，我還是會壓低音量、握緊拳頭，怕吵醒家人。
有些喜歡，就是這樣安靜地、一個人、跨越了半個地球和好多年。
zh-tw.editorNote: 傳給那個為了一支沒人懂的隊、獨自熬夜的真愛球迷
variations: 最深的熱愛往往最安靜，因為它不需要別人一起，就已經足夠。
跨越半個地球、跨越十幾年，我還是為同一件球衣熬夜。

en.content:
The team I support isn't my own country's — it's one I fell for by chance in a match over a decade ago.
Nobody stays up with me, the work chat never mentions it, and when they win there's no one to high-five.
3 AM, and it's just me and the glow of the TV in the living room.
But every time they score I still clench my fist and keep the volume low, so I don't wake the family.
Some kinds of love are just like this — quiet, alone, stretched across half the world and a whole lot of years.
en.editorNote: For the die-hard staying up alone for a team nobody around them gets
variations: The deepest love is often the quietest, because it doesn't need company to feel like enough.
Half a world away and a decade later, I'm still losing sleep over the same jersey.
